competency_groups.title: Conceptual Foundations and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of Chemistry, develop conceptual foundations, represent phenomena at macroscopic/molecular/symbolic levels, identify patterns, and apply knowledge to real-life situations.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty in measurement
Apply laws of chemical combination and Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition
Perform stoichiometry and stoichiometric calculations

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to trace the development from sub-atomic particles discovery through Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model, understanding atomic structure deeply.
competencies: Identify sub-atomic particles and their properties
Analyze historical developments leading to Bohr's Model of Atom
Apply Bohr's Model for Hydrogen atom calculations
Understand the transition towards Quantum Mechanical Model

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements, understand the genesis of periodic classification, apply modern periodic law, analyze electronic configurations across blocks (s, p, d, f), and predict trends in properties.
competencies: Justify need for element classification and explain Genesis of Periodic Classification
Apply Modern Periodic Law to determine the present form of Periodic Table
Nomenclature elements with atomic number > 100
Correlate electronic configuration with position in periodic table (s, p, d, f blocks)
Predict and explain periodic trends in properties
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) and hybridization concepts to explain molecular structure and properties.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach for Ionic/Electrovalent bonds
Analyze bond parameters
Predict molecular geometry using VSEPR Theory
Explain bonding via Valence Bond Theory and Hybridisation concepts
Describe Molecular Orbital Theory and bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand thermodynamic terms, measure enthalpy changes (calorimetry), analyze spontaneity using Gibbs Energy change, and relate equilibrium concepts.
competencies: Define thermodynamic terms and applications
Measure ∆U and ∆H via calorimetry

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium, understand factors affecting equilibria (Le Chatelier's principle), and solve ionic equilibrium problems.
competencies: Distinguish between physical and chemical dynamic equilibrium

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify oxidation-reduction reactions through classical ideas, electron transfer concepts, and oxidation numbers, linking them to electrode processes.
competencies: Identify oxidation and reduction in classical terms

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand tetravalence of carbon, shapes and representations of organic compounds, nomenclature rules (IUPAC), isomerism types, reaction mechanisms, and purification/analysis techniques.
competencies: Analyze structural representations and shapes of organic compounds
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons, understand properties of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic compounds, and assess carcinogenicity/toxicity.
competencies: Classify different types of hydrocarbons

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to express concentration, understand solubility principles (ideal/non-ideal), apply colligative properties for molar mass determination.
competencies: Express concentration of solutions

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Energy Conversion
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electrochemical cells, galvanic cells, Nernst equation applications, conductance, electrolytic processes, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion mechanisms.
competencies: Analyze Galvanic Cells and Electrolytic Cells

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze factors influencing reaction rates, apply integrated rate equations, understand temperature dependence (Arrhenius), and collision theory.
competencies: Analyze factors influencing rate of chemical reactions

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Elements Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to correlate position in periodic table with electronic configuration, analyze general properties of transition elements (d-block), lanthanoids, actinoids, and their applications.
competencies: Correlate electronic configuration with d/f block positions

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds and Bonding
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply Werner's theory, understand nomenclature rules, analyze isomerism types, explain bonding in coordination compounds (including metal carbonyls), and identify applications.
competencies: Apply Werner's Theory of Coordination Compounds

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify, name (nomenclature), understand C-X bond nature, methods of preparation, physical properties, chemical reactions, and polyhalogen compounds.
competencies: Analyze classification and nomenclature of haloalkanes/haloarenes

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify compounds, understand functional group structures (alcoholic/phenolic), nomenclature, and properties of commercially important alcohols.
competencies: Classify Alcohols, Phenols, and Ethers

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand nomenclature/structure of carbonyl groups (aldehydes/ketones) and carboxyl groups, methods of preparation, physical properties, chemical reactions, and uses.
competencies: Analyze structure and reactivity of Aldehydes/Ketones

competency_groups.title: Amines Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand structure/classification/nomenclature of amines, preparation methods (including Diazonium salts), physical properties, and chemical reactions in synthesis.
competencies: Analyze classification and nomenclature of Amines

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules Structure and Function
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify structures of carbohydrates, proteins (enzymes), vitamins, nucleic acids, hormones, and understand their biological roles.
competencies: Identify major classes of biomolecules

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry and Colloids
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to distinguish between true solutions, colloids, suspensions; understand physisorption/chemisorption factors, lyophilic/lyophobic properties, coagulation, emulsions.
competencies: Distinguish types of sols (Lyophilic/Lyophobic)

competency_groups.title: Isolation and Extraction Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand principles of extraction (concentration, oxidation, reduction), electrolytic methods, refining processes.
competencies: Analyze general principles of isolation
competency_groups.unit_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
competency_groups.chapter_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

competency_groups.title: Polymers Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand polymerisation processes, distinguish between homopolymers and copolymers.
competencies: Classify polymers as Homopolymers or Copolymers

competency_groups.title: Chemistry in Everyday Life
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify chemicals used in medicines (analgesics, antibiotics, etc.) and food preservatives/sweeteners/antioxidants.
competencies: Identify applications of chemistry in daily life

competency_groups.title: Laboratory Skills and Safety
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to perform basic laboratory techniques (cutting, bending glass), characterization/purification methods (melting/boiling point determination, crystallization), pH analysis, volumetric titrations, salt analysis, and qualitative organic tests.
competencies: Perform micro-chemical methods safely
competency_groups.chapter_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

competency_groups.title: Scientific Inquiry and Project Work
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to design scientific investigations, conduct laboratory testing, collect data from various sources (e.g., water contamination analysis, food adulteration studies), and analyze results.
competencies: Design and execute investigatory projects
